sections.paragraph:
συγκλεισμός, οῦ, ὁ (s. συγκλείω; pap, LXX) confinement, encirclement (Ezk 4:3, 7, 8; 5:2; 1 Macc 6:21; ViIs, ViEzk, ViHab, ViJer, et al. 3 [p. 69, 8 Sch.]) ἐν συγκλεισμῷ οὔσης τῆς πόλεως when the city was under siege 1 Cl 55:4; cp. vs. 5.—DELG s.v. κλείς.

pap pap = papyrus, -yri

LXX LXX = Septuaginta, ed. ARahlfs, unless otherwise specified—Lists 2, beg.

Sch. Sch. = TSchermann, Prophetarum vitae fabulosae—List 5

1 Cl 1 Cl = 1 Clement—List 1

  1. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 952
